user_query:
Build a minimal append-only event store with per-stream versioning and a global event log, then use it to capture and drive three workflows: (1) encode an agent identity fingerprint from markdown files, (2) generate an animal study protocol and a compliance checklist from a JSON config, and (3) produce Google Ads creative from context and performance data with an iteration summary.

Read all inputs from input/ and write all artifacts to output/ using the exact paths below.

Inputs to read:
- input/identity/SOUL.md (agent identity narrative)
- input/identity/MEMORY.md (agent historical behaviors and rules)
- input/study_config.json (animal study parameters)
- input/ad/context.json (ad platform, product, audience, constraints)
- input/ad/performance.csv (recent ad performance data for iteration)

Event store requirements:
- Implement output/event_store.py providing an Event class and an EventStore class with methods append(stream_id, stream_type, events, expected_version=None), read_stream(stream_id, from_version=0), and read_all(from_position=0, limit=1000).
- Enforce optimistic concurrency: append must check expected_version against the current last version of the stream and fail if mismatched.
- Maintain global_position strictly increasing across all events and per-stream version increments starting at 1.
- Emit an append-only global log at output/events.jsonl using an event envelope with fields: event_id, stream_id, stream_type, event_type, version, schema_version, data, metadata (must include timestamp and correlation_id), and global_position.
- Use exactly three streams for this task: Identity-001, Study-001, Ads-001.

Workflow events to append (at minimum):
- Identity-001: IdentityEncoded
- Study-001: ProtocolGenerated, ComplianceChecked
- Ads-001: CreativeGenerated, PerformanceAnalyzed, IterationLogged

Projections:
- Produce a rebuildable projection summarizing counts per event_type and the latest global_position at output/projections/activity_summary.json.

Workflow outputs:
1) Identity fingerprint
- Derive a compact identity fingerprint JSON by compressing the content of SOUL.md and MEMORY.md into structured keys (agent_name, source_files, core_values, behavioral_signatures, anti_patterns, voice_profile, encoded_at). Write to output/identity/identity_fingerprint.json.

2) Animal study protocol and compliance checklist
- Generate a publication-ready protocol from input/study_config.json and write to output/study/protocol.md.
- Generate a compliance checklist covering 10 essential items with statuses ("complete" or "missing"). Write to output/study/compliance.json with an array essential_10 of 10 items.

3) Google Ads creative and iteration report
- Generate bulk RSA-ready creative and write a CSV with columns headline_1, headline_2, headline_3, description_1, description_2, platform to output/ad/google_ads.csv. All headlines must be <=30 characters and all descriptions <=90 characters; set platform to "google_ads".
- Analyze input/ad/performance.csv, summarize winners/losers, list winning patterns, and document new angles being tested. Write the narrative report to output/ad/iteration.md.

Documentation:
- Write output/docs/design.md explaining event schema, optimistic concurrency strategy, global vs per-stream ordering, projection rebuildability, idempotency considerations, and schema evolution rules.
- Write output/README.md describing how to run your solution and how projections can be rebuilt from output/events.jsonl.

Important constraints:
- Events are immutable; never update or delete past events (append-only).
- Projections must be rebuildable solely from the global event log.
- Streams must be named exactly Identity-001, Study-001, Ads-001.
- All reads come from input/; all writes go under output/.
